steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The second sentence states Rogerson's 2015 calculation was more accurate than the 1920 one. The third sentence explains why the 1920 calculation was less accurate — it used rudimentary equipment. The blank introduces that explanation.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The logical relationship is concession/explanation. The blank needs a transition signaling that what follows explains why the earlier, less accurate result was predictable.
  • Choice A — "Likewise,": signals similarity between two things, but the 1920 calculation's flaws don't parallel Rogerson's accuracy — they explain the gap between them. ✗
  • Choice B — "To that end,": signals that what follows is done in pursuit of a stated goal, but explaining why old equipment was primitive isn't a goal-directed action. ✗
  • Choice C — "Of course,": signals that the following point is an unsurprising or natural explanation — it's obvious that primitive equipment would produce less accurate results. ✓
  • Choice D — "Ultimately,": signals a final outcome or conclusion, but the equipment note isn't a final outcome — it's a mid-explanation supporting fact. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
